microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user likely wants food warm but not excessively hot. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE this user values efficiency when rushed and accepts slightly cooler food. IF quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE extra warmth is acceptable when time pressure is low. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are intended to be served cold. IF social setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE social context does not outweigh the baseline preference for warm food. IF watching TV or working AND fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for comfort and warmth. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE this user's tie-break policy favors efficiency over perfect temperature when rushed.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food without a competing visual target THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user is not distraction-sensitive by default. IF social setting with partner on the left AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE preserving sightline to the partner matters in conversation. IF social setting with partner on the right AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user wants to keep that side visually open. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the screen is the main visual target. IF working on laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE task visibility is important. IF social setting with partner on one side AND fatigued AND any food THEN minimize that side occlusion BECAUSE this user consistently prioritizes social/visual access over convenience in shared settings. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE efficiency wins when no important visual target is present.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE the user is comfortable with normal motion but does not seek extremes. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND easy-to-handle food or drinking THEN fast BECAUSE this user shifts toward efficiency under time pressure. IF quiet personal setting AND relaxed with ample time AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE comfort can take priority when time is abundant. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE the user wants motions to remain socially comfortable without being too slow. IF social setting AND relaxed with ample time AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE slower motion feels less disruptive around others. IF any dining context AND fatigued AND feeding with limited-mouth-opening foods THEN slow BECAUSE smaller, more careful transfers are preferable when tired. IF working or watching TV AND in a hurry AND drinking or wiping THEN fast BECAUSE the tie-break policy favors efficiency when the task is simple and the user is rushed.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral AND bite-sized solid food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE this user has limited mouth opening and prefers narrower bites. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND long or wide foods that could create large bites THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE mouth-opening limits dominate. IF quiet personal setting AND in a hurry AND difficult slippery food THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this user accepts a larger bite when acquisition reliability becomes more important. IF social setting AND neutral AND difficult slippery food THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE avoiding repeated failed acquisitions is socially preferable. IF social setting AND fatigued AND difficult slippery food THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the tie-break policy favors reducing retries and effort when tired. IF any dining context AND relaxed with ample time AND easy food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE comfort and smaller bites matter more than speed. IF any dining context AND dessert or soft food prone to breaking when skewered narrowly THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE a more reliable pickup can outweigh the small-bite preference.
